mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and labor demand (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS exceed their 3-month highs.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, boosting S&P 500 returns through sectoral growth in construction and retail.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 35% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their 6-month peaks.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost consumer spending and drive positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) signals economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 60d returns in > 40% of periods where GDPC1 declines by > 1% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 indicates weak supply chain capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days through sectoral earnings disappointments.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through elevated consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 5d returns in > 40% of periods where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.
